description_long: This object is a standard urban traffic light mounted on a tall, slender pole that is anchored into a square concrete base with diagonal black and white striping for high visibility. The signal head features three distinct circular lenses arranged vertically, typical for red, yellow, and green lights, and is encased in a rectangular black housing with protruding visors to shield the lights from sunlight. The pole is ribbed for structural strength and aesthetic detail, and the base appears robust and heavy to prevent tipping or unauthorized movement. The overall design is utilitarian, prioritizing visibility and durability in outdoor urban environments. No pedestrian signals or control boxes are visible, and the object is intended to be a fixed, non-movable installation. The materials suggest a combination of metal for the pole and signal housing, glass or polycarbonate for the lenses, and concrete for the base.
description: A vertical, pole-mounted traffic light with three circular signal lenses, set on a striped concrete base for urban street use.
description_view_0: Front view showing the three vertically stacked signal lights and the striped concrete base.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the protruding visors of the signal lights and the depth of the signal housing.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back of the signal housing and the ribbed pole, with no visible lights.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the profile of the signal lights and the mounting structure.
